question: Does the generated output contain at least 4 distinct interleaved segments (e.g., Image 1, Text 1, Image 2, Text 2)?
Is each composite image placed immediately adjacent to its corresponding explanatory text?
Does the first generated composite image preserve the key elements of the 'mountaintop portrait' source (person in red jacket, rocky peak, sea of clouds)?
Does the second generated composite image preserve the key elements of the 'wheat field portrait' source (woman in white dress, golden wheat field)?
Does the mountaintop composite feature a giant animal added to the sky area, maintaining the scale logic established by the example?
Does the wheat field composite feature a giant animal added to the sky area, maintaining the scale logic established by the example?
Is the animal chosen for the mountaintop composite strictly NOT a whale?
Is the animal chosen for the wheat field composite strictly NOT a whale?
Are the animals chosen for the two scenes distinct from each other (no repetition of the same species)?
Does the added animal in the mountaintop composite clearly exhibit a semi-transparent visual style?
Does the added animal in the wheat field composite clearly exhibit a semi-transparent visual style?
Does the added animal in the mountaintop composite feature glowing or luminous edges?
Does the added animal in the wheat field composite feature glowing or luminous edges?
Does the text accompanying the mountaintop composite explicitly identify the animal used and explain why it fits the scene visually or atmospherically?
Does the text accompanying the wheat field composite explicitly identify the animal used and explain why it fits the scene visually or atmospherically?
Does the animal shown in the mountaintop composite image semantically match the species named in the accompanying text?
Does the animal shown in the wheat field composite image semantically match the species named in the accompanying text?
Do both generated composites maintain a consistent surreal/dreamlike artistic style (transparency, glow, scale) comparable to the reference whale image?
Are the generated composite images free of significant visual artifacts, malformed anatomy, or broken geometry?
